status_summary: guile-asmsim.scm is relevant command-semantics evidence, but it diverges from the formal command table and the older RAA/SEMSIM/FSMSIM sketches. AS should not use it as direct executable authority for standard-signal or write-buffer command tokens.

special_message_status.summary: Unlike RAA, SEMSIM, and FSMSIM, this witness names only init-family special messages and omits write-buffer names as well as standard-signal.

standard_signal_status.summary: Standard signals remain ordinary binary input in the role-step clauses, while process-buffer appends numeric standard-signals to the command list. This does not resolve whether a standard-signal command token should replay ordinary binary-input behavior.

write_buffer_status.summary: The witness has a write-buf helper over binary 0/1 values and a self-mailbox numeric append path, but it does not define named write-buf-zero or write-buf-one command tokens.

command_buffer_status.as_interpretation: The process-buffer command list diverges from the formal named command table and has an index-shape ambiguity at the list-ref/list-tail boundary. It is evidence of unresolved command representation, not a stable execution rule.

as_boundary: Keep standard-signal and write-buffer command-token execution blocked until a later ADR selects a source-backed command representation, target mapping, and clearing boundary.
